columns.extraction_plan: This column represents an overall risk judgment, which is not explicitly reported in this PDF. The PDF presents data and results, but doesn't include an assessment of the risk of bias or other factors that would contribute to an overall risk judgment.
From Table 1, the column "ADT Alone (N=393)" reports the number of participants in the control arm (ADT alone). Extract the value "393".

The study investigates "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer". The abstract states: "We assessed whether concomitant treatment with ADT plus docetaxel would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone." Therefore, the add-on treatment is docetaxel.
The control arm is "ADT alone". This is mentioned in the abstract: "We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone."
Extract the value '64' from Table 1, row 'Age - yr', column 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'.
Extract the value '63' from Table 1, row 'Age - yr', column 'ADT Alone (N=393)'.

plans.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

columns.column_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
columns.extraction_plan: The abstract states "We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone." Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 is ADT plus docetaxel. ADT is Androgen-deprivation therapy, which is a hormonal therapy. Docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent. Therefore, the Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1 is "Chemotherapy and Hormonal Therapy".

Approximately 86% of the 390 patients in the combination group who started the assigned therapy completed six cycles of docetaxel therapy (Table S2 in the Supplementary Appendix), and approximately 74% of all the treated patients received all planned cycles without dose modifications. Extract "86% of the 390 patients"

At the time of this analysis, castration-resistant prostate cancer with at least biochemical progression had developed in 287 patients assigned to ADT alone; 137 patients of these patients had received docetaxel for castration-resistant prostate cancer, and another 10 patients had received docetaxel before castration resistance was confirmed. Extract "137 patients of these patients had received docetaxel for castration-resistant prostate cancer, and another 10 patients had received docetaxel before castration resistance was confirmed"

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 provides a list of Grade 3 or higher adverse events among the 390 patients who received the docetaxel-containing regimen. The table lists the number and percentage of patients for each specific adverse event. To get the total, sum the number of patients experiencing each Grade 3 or higher event. For example, 7 (1.8%) had allergic reaction Grade 3, 1 (0.3%) had allergic reaction Grade 4, etc. The total number of patients experiencing any event is 65 (16.7%) for Grade 3, 49 (12.6%) for Grade 4, and 1 (0.3%) for Grade 5.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The article states "Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported." Therefore, a comprehensive list of all Grade 3 or higher adverse events in the control arm is not available.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 provides a list of Grade 3 or higher adverse events among the 390 patients who received the docetaxel-containing regimen. The table lists the number and percentage of patients for each specific adverse event. The attribution of relatedness to study therapy was made by the local investigators. To get the total, sum the number of patients experiencing each Grade 3 or higher event. For example, 7 (1.8%) had allergic reaction Grade 3, 1 (0.3%) had allergic reaction Grade 4, etc. The total number of patients experiencing any event is 65 (16.7%) for Grade 3, 49 (12.6%) for Grade 4, and 1 (0.3%) for Grade 5.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The article states "Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported." Therefore, a comprehensive list of treatment-related Grade 3 or higher adverse events in the control arm is not available.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Table 3 indicates that 1 (0.3%) patient experienced a Grade 5 adverse event (sudden death) in the treatment arm, which was considered possibly related to docetaxel.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The article states "Adverse events among patients assigned to ADT alone were not routinely documented, although major adverse events were to be reported." Therefore, a comprehensive list of treatment-related Grade 5 adverse events in the control arm is not available.

Not reported. The PDF focuses on survival rates, disease progression, and adverse events, but does not mention any quality of life assessments.

The primary objective was "to test the hypothesis that the median overall survival would be 33.3% longer among patients receiving docetaxel added to ADT early during therapy than among patients receiving ADT alone." This is stated in the "METHODS" section of the abstract.

The PDF does not explicitly mention the use of a standardized "Quality of Life Scale" to assess participants' well-being and satisfaction with their health status. While adverse events and toxic effects are monitored, there's no indication of a formal quality of life assessment using a specific scale.

The column 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)' reports the number of participants in the ADT plus Docetaxel arm as 397. The column 'ADT Alone (N=393)' reports the number of participants in the ADT Alone arm as 393.
Page 8, Table 2: Extract the 'Median' value from the row labeled 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer - mo*' under the column 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'. The value is 20.2.
Page 8, Table 2: Extract the 'Median' value from the row labeled 'Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer - mo*' under the column 'ADT Alone (N=393)'. The value is 11.7.

The abstract mentions a secondary objective: 'The median time to biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic progression was 20.2 months in the combination group, as compared with 11.7 months in the ADT-alone group (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.72; P<0.001). The rate of a prostate-specific antigen level of less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 12 months was 27.7% in the combination group versus 16.8% in the ADT-alone group (P<0.001).'

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: Not reported. The PDF does not explicitly mention or analyze results based on synchronous metastases.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: Not reported. The PDF does not explicitly mention or analyze results based on metachronous metastases.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The study prospectively stratified patients according to high or low volume of metastatic disease. Figure 1B (page 6) shows Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival for patients with high-volume disease. Table 1 (page 5) shows the number of patients with high vs low volume metastases at baseline. Figure 2 (page 7) shows hazard ratios for death in subgroups, including high vs low volume.

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: Yes. The study prospectively stratified patients according to high or low volume of metastatic disease. Figure 1C (page 6) shows Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival for patients with low-volume disease. Table 1 (page 5) shows the number of patients with high vs low volume metastases at baseline. Figure 2 (page 7) shows hazard ratios for death in subgroups, including high vs low volume.
The study is referred to as the "E3805 study" in the "METHODS" section.
The treatment arms are 'ADT plus docetaxel' and 'ADT alone'. These are mentioned in the abstract and throughout the paper.
The study compares two treatment arms: "ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles)" and "ADT alone".
